profile.personality: A sensitive, chilly individual prone to chronic catarrhal conditions that worsen in cold weather. Despite being cold-natured constitutionally, once ill their complaints paradoxically improve in cool air and worsen from warmth, reflecting an inner contradictory nature.
profile.mental_state: Mental symptoms are sparse but center on depression and cognitive sluggishness, with headaches worse from mental exertion. There is a characteristic waking state with basilar or frontal headache, and mental faculties are dulled under a pervasive sense of gloom described as a cloud hanging over everything.
profile.emotional_pattern: The core emotional state is deep sadness and apprehensiveness, with anxiety most pronounced between 2 and 4 A.M. This melancholic disposition may unpredictably alternate with periods of loquacity and liveliness, resembling the oscillating emotional pattern seen in Lachesis.

rubric_passages.Bladder: Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
rubric_passages.Generalities: The patient is cold, and his complaints come on in cold weather. He is sensitive to cold, easily takes cold. But you must distinguish between the patient himself and his complaints, because they are entirely opposite to each other. When he once becomes sick from exposure to cold, he is always worse in a warm room and better in the cold air.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: Uterine hemorrhage is a strong feature of this remedy. Copious, frequent, prolonged menstrual flow. Large, hard, black clots fill the uterus, are expelled by labor-like pains, and form again. Inflammation of uterus and vagina, with copious, thick, white, jelly-like, ropy mucus. Soreness of the vulva; cannot bear the pressure of the clothing.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat, sensation of: There is a general hyperesthesia of the skin and mucous membranes. Sensitive to the pressure of the clothing.
rubric_passages.Throat: Sore throat with redness. Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx. Fauces very sensitive. Arch of palate and fauces, as far as visible, very red. Burning in the throat < in the warmth, especially when warmed up in bed, < from warm drinks, though hot drinks are not so bad. Better from cold drinks.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat: His cough is brought on in a warm room; from being too warm in bed; from drinking warm things. It is better from drinking cold things in a cold room; worse from exertion; from getting heated up; from becoming warm; that is, after the complaint has once set in, it reverses itself.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female, menses: Copious, frequent, prolonged menstrual flow. Large, hard, black clots fill the uterus, are expelled by labor-like pains, and form again.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
rubric_passages.Urine: Albumen in the urine. Dark red sediment in the urine. Pain shooting from the kidney to the bladder and down the legs; < from motion.
rubric_passages.Cough: Spasmodic cough; whooping cough; the cough of drunkards. The chronic catarrhal state of the Coccus cacti patient comes on especially in the winter. It comes on when the cold weather begins and lasts till the warm weather comes.
rubric_passages.Expectoration: Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke. This is due to the fact that the mucus is so tenacious that it cannot be ejected from the pharynx in the usual way, therefore he must vomit.
rubric_passages.Cough, tickling: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
rubric_passages.Expectoration, viscid: Great quantities of this mucus form in the nose, in the throat, in the air passages generally, and in the vagina. Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea, irritation, larynx: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. The slightest touch on the palate or even the gums in examination of the throat will cause gagging sometimes when the parts look normal.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea, tickling in the air: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
rubric_passages.Nose: Discharge of thick yellow mucus from the nose; nose stopped up, with inclination to sneeze. Great dryness of the nose.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea, irritation: Anything coming in contact with the pharynx, the inside of the mouth, or even the gums, produces gagging and retching and will bring on the cough.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea, tickling: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea, larynx in general: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
rubric_passages.Bladder, urging to urinate (morbid desir: Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
rubric_passages.Cough, hacking, tickling in larynx, from: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx. He cannot hawk without gagging.
rubric_passages.Cough, irritation in air passages, from: Anything coming in contact with the pharynx, the inside of the mouth, or even the gums, produces gagging and retching and will bring on the cough.
rubric_passages.Cough, irritation in air passages, from,: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough.
rubric_passages.Cough, night: They are apt to be worse at night when he becomes warm in bed. If he can lie in a cool room without much covering he will go longer without coughing.
rubric_passages.Cough, paroxysmal: It is a spasmodic cough, forcing the patient into the most violent efforts. The face becomes purple. Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke.

profile.personality: An extremely sensitive, high-strung individual with heightened perception across all senses—hearing, sight, touch, and smell. Quick to think and act, overflowing with ideas, mentally sharp and capable of rapid comprehension and debate. Often a habitual coffee or stimulant user who pushes through ordeals with nervous energy, then collapses into exhaustion.
profile.mental_state: The mind is hyperactive and flooded with thoughts, making sleep impossible; plans, memories, poetry from childhood, and fanciful visions pour in unbidden. There is increased mental capacity and easy comprehension followed by a reactive state of stupor and sleepiness. Hearing is so acute that distant clocks, barking dogs, and even imaginary sounds are perceived, and all noise intensifies suffering.
profile.emotional_pattern: Emotions are the primary trigger for illness—especially joy, pleasant surprises, and excited anticipation, which produce nervous breakdown, sleeplessness, and neuralgia. There is pitiful weeping from pain or the slightest neglect, with hurt feelings, hysteria, and a tendency to faint from sudden emotions. Fear of death accompanies acute episodes, and the patient demands absolute stillness and quiet from everyone around them.

rubric_passages.Chest: Strong, quick palpitation of the heart with extreme nervousness, sleeplessness and cerebral erethism caused by unexpected news of great good fortune.
rubric_passages.Chill: He is extremely sensitive to cold, sensitive to the wind and cold weather. Complaints come on in the cold weather, from the cold air.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Coffea has a painful sensitiveness of the skin beyond comprehension. I remember one particular case. A woman had her lower limb out of bed and it was as red as fire down one side.
rubric_passages.Generalities: This drug is characterized by a general sensitivity. Sensitiveness of vision, of hearing, of smell, of touch; sensitiveness to pain.
rubric_passages.Head: Headache as if the whole brain were torn and bruised, or dashed to pieces. Worse from motion, noise or light.
rubric_passages.Mind: Fainting from sudden emotions. Hysteria, nervousness, weeping. Pitiful weeping from pain trembling and weeping from hurt feelings; the slightest neglect. The greatest mental and physical exhaustion; great restlessness lying awake most of the night.
rubric_passages.Mind, irritability: Pitiful weeping from pain trembling and weeping from hurt feelings; the slightest neglect.
rubric_passages.Mouth: Pain in the mouth and jaws, better from holding ice-cold water in the mouth.
rubric_passages.Rectum: The first effect of Opium is to constipate. Let several doses be given, and as the effects of the Opium wear off he may have diarrhea.
rubric_passages.Sleep: The wakefulness produced by Coffea is well known, even to the laity. It is taken by nurses to keep them awake nights with their patients. The Coffea patient is quick to act and to think. So full of ideas that she lies awake nights making plans, thinking of a thousand things; utterly unable to banish the thoughts that flood the mind.
rubric_passages.Generalities, faintness: Her heart palpitates, her pulse flickers, she has fainting spells, and without Coffea she may die.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: Pains are increased by noise. Sensitiveness of hearing is so great that sounds are painful. Pains in the face, toothache, headache; pains in the lower limbs; everywhere aggravated by noise.
rubric_passages.Head, congestion: At times the head is hot, the face is flushed and, the eyes so brilliant that one fears apoplexy.
rubric_passages.Mind, restlessness: The greatest mental and physical exhaustion; great restlessness lying awake most of the night.
rubric_passages.Clinical: Neuralgic toothache entirely relieved by holding cold water in the mouth, returning as it becomes warm. Toothache during the menstrual period. Complaints of anemic children during dentition.
rubric_passages.Abdomen: Worse from every motion, with violent pain in the groins, and fear of death.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: Palpitation of the heart, pulse fluttering. Strong, quick palpitation of the heart with extreme nervousness, sleeplessness and cerebral erethism caused by unexpected news of great good fortune.
rubric_passages.Face: The neuralgia of the face is a common feature of old coffee drinkers.
rubric_passages.Face, heat: Eyes brilliant; pupils dilated; face flushed head hot.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat, sensation of: Intense stinging, burning pains in the skin with the redness and heat with coarse rash coming on suddenly, leaving just as suddenly.
rubric_passages.Head, pain: Headache as if the whole brain were torn and bruised, or dashed to pieces. Worse from motion, noise or light. Headache intolerable. Head feels small, and as if filled with fluid. Nervous hysterical headache. One-sided headache.
rubric_passages.Sleep, sleeplessness: So full of ideas that she lies awake nights making plans, thinking of a thousand things; utterly unable to banish the thoughts that flood the mind; hears the clocks on the distant steeples.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat: Hot head; inflamed condition of the gums. The head is hot, the face is flushed.

profile.personality: The Colchicum personality is profoundly exhausted, depleted, and averse to all forms of exertion. They are chilly, sensitive individuals who dread movement and disturbance, preferring to lie still and be left alone. There is a deep irritability born of weakness, with an almost paralytic resignation to their suffering.
profile.mental_state: Mental confusion and disordered comprehension dominate — they cannot understand what they read and struggle to process information clearly. There is delirium in advanced states, with depression of mind and a heightened sensitivity to pain that seems to register as much in the mind as in the body. The mental faculties sink in proportion to the physical prostration.
profile.emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of intense, almost visceral aversion — particularly a hatred of food odors that becomes so consuming it takes possession of the entire person. This perverted sensitivity extends to a general irritability and hypersensitivity to all sensory impressions, with deep anxiety accompanying the progressive weakness. There is a quality of sinking despair, as though life itself is flowing out of them with every exertion.

rubric_passages.Chest: Stinging, tearing pains in the muscles of the chest.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, palpitation: Palpitation; oppression of the chest. Feels as if he had a great weight on the chest; cannot breathe.
rubric_passages.Chill: He is a chilly patient, sensitive to cold.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Numbness, oedema, swelling of the limbs.
rubric_passages.Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the limbs or of one limb, or of any part.
rubric_passages.Generalities: All the complaints of this remedy are aggravated from motion. The painful complaints, the head complaints, the bowel complaints. the liver complaints, the stomach complaints, are all worse from motion.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness, enervation: Great weakness and exhaustion. He cannot exert himself in the least without causing dyspnea, Must lie down; does not want to move; sinking of strength; seems as if his life will flow out of him from motion and from exertion; so tired and exhausted.
rubric_passages.Head: The headaches are all of a rheumatic character. Very often the whole skull, the pericranium, is sore as if bruised. The scalp is sensitive. Pressure in the head- constriction; pressing, bursting headaches. Heat in the head. Tearing in the scalp. Headaches are all aggravated by motion.
rubric_passages.Mind: Colchicum has delirium, prostration, depression of mind, great sensitiveness to pain, which he seems to feel in his mind, and it brings out mental symptoms. Very sensitive to pain; confusion of the mind; disorders of comprehension. Cannot understand what he reads.
rubric_passages.Rectum: Great relaxation of the parts. Protrusion of the rectum.
rubric_passages.Rectum, pain: Very painful, extremely painful is the Colchicum stool. Great soreness in the abdomen.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Nausea, gagging, retching at the bare mention of food in his presence. The thought and smell of food bring on nausea and vomiting.
rubric_passages.Appetite, aversion, meat: The smell of fish, eggs, fat meats or broths causes nausea even unto faintness.
rubric_passages.Stomach, distension: The abdomen is distended with flatus, tympanitic. Great soreness in the whole abdomen just such a tympanitic condition as we have in typhoid.
rubric_passages.Stomach, pain: In the stomach there is sometimes coldness and sometimes burning.
rubric_passages.Stomach, pain, burning: Burning in the pit of the stomach.
rubric_passages.Stomach, thirst: The Colchicum patient may have much thirst or no thirst, or these may alternate.
rubric_passages.Abdomen, pain: Spasmodic pains, colic, tearing pains, burning, griping pains, forcing the patient to bend double. Aggravated from motion. Great tenderness and soreness with the colic. Aggravated from eating; ameliorated from bending double.
rubric_passages.Generalities, faintness: The smell of fish, eggs, fat meats or broths causes nausea even unto faintness.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: Touch and motion bring on a painful sensation in the body as of electric vibrations.
rubric_passages.Clinical, paralysis: A paralytic weakness was observed in the poisonous effects and too prolonged provings. The jaw hangs down, the muscles are flabby, relaxed. Paralysis of the limbs or of one limb, or of any part.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness: Great prostration accompanies the complaints of this remedy. Weakness of the limbs, great exhaustion, nervous exhaustion of a typhoid character. He gradually grows weaker like one going into Bright's disease.
rubric_passages.Appetite: Absolutely unable to touch food. Nausea, gagging, retching at the bare mention of food in his presence.
rubric_passages.Appetite, aversion: Aversion to food; loathing the sight and smell, more the smell of it.
rubric_passages.Clinical: Rheumatic troubles that have been going on for some time, will end in cardiac troubles. When cardiac troubles with valvular defects are present, almost the first thing the busy doctor thinks of is a history of rheumatism.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, heart: Heart's action muffled, indistinct, very weak.

profile.personality: A person worn down by prolonged vexations and annoyances, haughty and easily offended or chagrined. Not a strong, vigorous type who suddenly falls ill, but rather one whose constitution has been weakened by chronic irritations, nervous exhaustion, and often habits of overeating.
profile.mental_state: Irritable and disinclined to talk, answer questions, or see friends — wants to be left alone. Increasingly anxious as suffering intensifies, screaming with pain and pacing restlessly despite profound weakness. The mental state resembles someone who has labored for years under business failures or domestic betrayals, producing nervous exhaustion.
profile.emotional_pattern: The core emotional trigger is anger with indignation — a sense of being wronged or affronted that directly precipitates physical symptoms. Even minor provocations cause disproportionate upset in a person already sensitized by accumulated grievances. The emotional landscape alternates between suppressed fury, restless desperation, and a deep desire for solitary withdrawal.

rubric_passages.Abdomen, hard: The pains in the lower part of the abdomen are relieved by drawing up the limbs and pressing with the fists.
rubric_passages.Back: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
rubric_passages.Back, pain: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
rubric_passages.Back, pain, dorsal region: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Where the pain is better from hard pressure and from heat, where it is worse during repose and drives the patient to despair, Colocynth will generally cure.
rubric_passages.Generalities: The principal feature of Colocynth is its severe, tearing, neuralgic pains; so severe that the patient is unable to keep still. Sometimes they are > by motion - at least it appears that they are worse during rest- > by pressure and sometimes > by heat.
Sometimes they are > by motion - at least it appears that they are worse during rest- > by pressure and sometimes > by heat.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: In the violent ovarian neuralgias of Colocynth, the woman will flex the limb of the painful side hard against the abdomen and hold it there.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female, pain: In the violent ovarian neuralgias of Colocynth, the woman will flex the limb of the painful side hard against the abdomen and hold it there.
rubric_passages.Head: Painful, tearing, digging through the whole brain, becoming unbearable when moving the eyelids. Intense pain through the whole head; worse from moving the eyes. Severe, pressing, tearing headache, causing her to cry out.
rubric_passages.Mind: As soon as the prover of Colocynth begins to have pains along the course of nerves he becomes irritable; everything vexes him; he is worse from vexation.
rubric_passages.Mind, irritability: As soon as the prover of Colocynth begins to have pains along the course of nerves he becomes irritable; everything vexes him; he is worse from vexation.
rubric_passages.Mind, restlessness, nervousness: Walks about the room and becomes increasingly anxious as the pain goes on.
rubric_passages.Rectum: Diarrhoea and dysentery from anger with indignation; the most awful tenesmus during stool; urging to stool with colic.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Aversion to food. Violent thirst. Colic brought on from drinking while overheated; from eating indigestible things, from high living; colic from eating potatoes.
rubric_passages.Stomach, pain: The stomach pains are clutching, cramping and digging; as if grasped by the fingers.
rubric_passages.Abdomen, pain: Similar pains occur lower down in the abdomen, but they are still better from hard pressure, and from doubling up which amounts to pressure, come on in paroxysms of increasing severity, until the patient is nauseated and vomits, and are associated with great restlessness and faint, sinking feeling at the pit of the stomach.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: The principal feature of Colocynth is its severe, tearing, neuralgic pains; so severe that the patient is unable to keep still.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pressure: All pains are better from pressure, but this is in the beginning. After the pain has been going for several days with increasing severity, the part becomes very sensitive and pressure cannot be endured.
rubric_passages.Generalities, side: They are generally on the left side; while those of Belladonna are on the right, and are caused by cold.
rubric_passages.Mind, restlessness: In spite of extreme restlessness there is great weakness with the pains.

profile.personality: A slow, passive, indifferent constitution that progressively withdraws from engagement with life and other people. The Conium type is hypochondriacal, morose, and averse to company, yet paradoxically irritable when others attempt to reason with them or offer comfort. They are fundamentally broken-down individuals whose vital force is in steady decline, manifesting as both physical and mental exhaustion.
profile.mental_state: The mind is characterized by progressive deterioration—inability to concentrate, failing memory, and a gradual drift toward imbecility rather than acute insanity. Mental effort is unbearable; the patient cannot rivet attention, cannot meditate, and sits lost in thought with confused feelings. There is a slow-forming weakness of mind that the family barely notices until the accumulation of strange behaviors and lapses becomes undeniable.
profile.emotional_pattern: Deep sadness and gloom predominate, with the patient sitting and moping in corners without apparent reason, recurring in periodic waves (notably on a fourteen-day cycle). Every form of excitement—emotional, social, or from stimulants—brings on distress, weakness, and deeper depression. Grief and suppressed desires (particularly sexual deprivation in widows and widowers) are core triggers, producing a state of trembling, weeping helplessness and a pervasive sense that the future holds only sickness, sorrow, and decline.

rubric_passages.Back: In the back, weakness is a most striking thing, with some dorsal pains, Lancinating pains are spoken of.
rubric_passages.Back, pain: After injuries, especially in the lumbar region, pains and filling up of the veins of the lower limbs.
rubric_passages.Bladder: The bladder is weak, can expel only a part of the urine. Or sometimes there is a paralytic condition and no expulsive power.
rubric_passages.Chest: In the chest, violent stitches. Painful swelling of the breasts. Rending, tearing pains in the chest.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, palpitation: After every stool tremulous weakness and palpitation.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Rheumatic pains; paralysis of the lower limbs; ulceration. And the sufferings and conditions are better by letting the limbs hang down.
rubric_passages.Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the legs and hip.
rubric_passages.Extremities, paralysis, painless: A great many of the complaints are painless. The ulcers and the paralytic conditions are painless.
rubric_passages.Generalities: Trembling, jerking of the muscles and twitching from the weakness of the nerves. Inability to stand any physical effort without great exhaustion.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness, enervation: Gradually growing paralytic weakness, somewhat as was described in Cocculus. Exhaustion of body and mind, that is a general slowing down of all the activities of the body.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: In the woman induration and enlargement of the ovaries and uterus.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female, pain: Burning, stinging, tearing pains in the neck of the uterus.
rubric_passages.Head: There are many headaches in these patients. Patients going into decline will manifest headaches. Stitching, tearing pains in the head; throbbing in the head.
rubric_passages.Mind: Conium has such a deep action that it gradually brings about a state of imbecility. The mind gives out. The mind at first becomes tired like the muscles of the body. Unable to sustain any mental effort. The memory is weak.
rubric_passages.Mind, irritability: Every thing vexes and disturbs him. Cannot endure any kind of excitement, it brings on physical and mental distress, brings on weakness and sadness.
rubric_passages.Rectum: Often more common than diarrhoea is constipation with ineffectual urging, hard cool, paralysis of the rectum.
rubric_passages.Rectum, constipation: Often more common than diarrhoea is constipation with ineffectual urging, hard cool, paralysis of the rectum. Inability to strain at cool, inability to expel contents because of the paralytic weakness of all the muscles that take part in expulsion.
rubric_passages.Rectum, pain: Pulsation and emptiness in the abdomen after a normal stool.
rubric_passages.Skin: Nodules in the breast even where milk has not yet formed; lumps and nodules, indurations and enlarged glands form under the skin all over the body.
rubric_passages.Sleep: Another grand feature of the remedy; he sweats copiously during sleep. Sometimes the patient will say that if he merely closes the eyes he will sweat. It is certainly true on closing the eyes preparatory to going to sleep he will break out in a sweat.
rubric_passages.Stomach: There are many stomach troubles; ulceration of the stomach; cancer of the stomach. Conium is one of the greatest palliatives in symptoms of the stomach when all the symptoms agree.
rubric_passages.Stomach, eructations: Fullness in throat with suppressed eructations.
rubric_passages.Stomach, pain: Hardness of the abdomen, great sensitiveness of the abdomen. Pinching pains, stitching pains, colicky, cutting pains, cramping pains.
rubric_passages.Vertigo: Vertigo when turning the head, like turning in a circle, when rising from a seat; worse when lying down, as though the bed were turning in a circle; when turning in bed or when looking around.

profile.personality: A deeply prostrated, besotted individual who appears intoxicated and sluggish, with slow passive movements and a tendency toward mental confusion. They are highly sensitive to their surroundings, easily irritated by the slightest annoyance, and may crave intoxicating drinks with an inability to resist the craving.
profile.mental_state: The mind is clouded with a low, passive delirium characterized by mumbling, stumbling over words, and clumsy loquacity rather than wild excitement. Cognitive function is markedly impaired — they cannot add figures, make mistakes in writing, transpose letters and sentences, and are unable to manage their own affairs. Suspicion of friends develops alongside an inability to reason on a rational basis.
profile.emotional_pattern: A pervasive melancholy dominates, with thoughts dwelling continually on death, accompanied by timidity and deep-seated fear. They are excessively sensitive, easily moved to tears by reading, yet paradoxically cross and infuriated by the least annoyance. An underlying anxious pallor with cold sweat alternates with periods of irritability and emotional volatility.

rubric_passages.Back: Carbuncles that come upon the neck and upon the back begin with a pustule, and then several come and they are surrounded by little pustules and papules and there is pitting upon pressure.
rubric_passages.Generalities: An awful state of nervousness prevails. Trembling of the limbs, tremulous weakness. On protruding the tongue it comes out quivering. Tired by the slightest exertion. Sudden prostration of the vital powers. A paralytic weakness prevails throughout.
rubric_passages.Generalities, faintness, fainting: A mental and physical prostration that is almost paralytic in character.
rubric_passages.Generalities, side, right: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: Hemorrhages, Either dark clots or blood that has no tendency to coagulate and keeps on flowing. There is great trouble at the climacteric period. Hot flashes. Jaundice. Hemorrhage from the uterus or from other parts. Cancer of the uterus with much bleeding.
rubric_passages.Mouth: Inflammation of the gums. Bleeding from the mouth. Inflammation of the throat with bleeding of the throat. Burning in the throat and mouth, Trembling, quivering and swollen tongue. Trembling of the tongue when it is put out.
rubric_passages.Rectum: Stool black, thin, like coffee grounds. Dysentery of septic origin from foul water, food, etc. Diarrhoea from noxious effluvia.
rubric_passages.Skin: Boils, carbuncles and eruptions are surrounded by a purplish condition of the skin, a mottled, blue, splotched or marbled state. It produces boils, abscesses and a condition somewhat resembling a carbuncle, with burning and violent pains, but the peculiar feature is the doughy centre. Around the boil or carbuncle for many inches there is oedema, with pitting upon pressure.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Pain in the stomach; coldness as if a piece of ice were in the stomach or in the abdomen. Stomach irritable, unable to retain anything, constantly throws up blood.
rubric_passages.Generalities, faintness: A mental and physical prostration that is almost paralytic in character. Sudden prostration of the vital powers.
rubric_passages.Generalities, side: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness: Tremulous weakness. Tired by the slightest exertion. Sudden prostration of the vital powers. A paralytic weakness prevails throughout.
rubric_passages.Appetite: He craves intoxicating drinks and is unable to resist the craving. This wonderful resemblance to old inebriates has led to the use of Crotalus in delirium tremens; it has the besotted countenance, the purple aspect of the face, the peculiar kind of hunger in the drunkard, the craving by spells for stimulants.
rubric_passages.Appetite, desires: He craves intoxicating drinks and is unable to resist the craving.
rubric_passages.Clinical: Crotalus is indicated in disease of the very lowest, the most putrid type, coming on with unusual rapidity, reaching that putrid state in an unusually short time. Crotalus has cured ulceration of the stomach. It has greatly restrained the growth of carcinoma when there is much vomiting of bile and blood.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: There is some reason to think that this will be more or less a heart remedy from the great cardiac weakness it produces. But the other snake poisons like Naja, Lach. and Elaps have had more clinical application than this one. This one seems to prostrate the heart, but also to prostrate the whole body.
rubric_passages.Ear: Surging in the ears. Sensitive to noises. Dull aching and throbbing in the ears. Foetid, copious, yellow, offensive, bloody discharges from the ears.
rubric_passages.Stool: Stool black, thin, like coffee grounds. Dysentery of septic origin from foul water, food, etc. Diarrhoea from noxious effluvia.
rubric_passages.Throat: Inflammation of the throat with bleeding of the throat. Burning in the throat and mouth. The throat will be filled up under such circumstances with a diphtheritic membrane that looks dark. There is bleeding all around it.
rubric_passages.Ear, discharges: Foetid, copious, yellow, offensive, bloody discharges from the ears. Blood oozing from the ears in drops in zymotic diseases, low forms of scarlet fever, or of diphtheria where there is oozing from the eyes and ears.
rubric_passages.Eye: Hemorrhage from the eyes. Yellow appearance of the eyes. Blood exudes from the eye, burning in eyes; redness with lachrymation. Pressure in the eyes as if the eyes would be pushed out from the head. Paralysis of the upper lids.
rubric_passages.Mouth, protruded, tongue: Trembling, quivering and swollen tongue. Trembling of the tongue when it is put out.
rubric_passages.Stomach, nausea: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting. This is a wonderfully bilious remedy, sick headaches, vomiting of bile in great quantities.
rubric_passages.Stomach, vomiting: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting. The various low forms of disease calling for Crotalus often, begin with vomiting great quantities of bile, sometimes bile mixed with blood. Stomach irritable, unable to retain anything, constantly throws up blood.

profile.personality: A restless, sensitive constitution that requires an outlet or 'vent' for internal disturbance. The Croton Tiglium type exhibits an alternating pattern—suppression inward when external expression clears, and eruption outward when internal symptoms abate—suggesting a personality caught between containment and explosive release.
profile.mental_state: Mental clarity is disrupted by physical intensity: vanishing of sight, vertigo, and overwhelming nausea cloud cognition during acute states. The mind is dominated by the body's violent, sudden discharges and the relentless pull of internal tension, as expressed by the characteristic sensation of being drawn inward 'as with a string.'
profile.emotional_pattern: Emotional suffering is intense, prolonged, and endured stoically—tears come only when pain becomes unbearable, as seen in the nursing mother who paced the floor for several nights before seeking help. There is a deep sensitivity to disturbance, where the slightest provocation (a mouthful of milk, a breath of air) triggers an overwhelming, explosive response, reflecting an emotional threshold that is low yet whose expression is sudden and forceful.

rubric_passages.Chest: After confinement the mother may go on a little while with all things following normally, but all at once she commences to have pains in one or the other mammary gland, and the drawing as with a string comes up again. It feels to her as if a string were attached behind the nipple pulling backward, a sharp, drawing, stinging pain that will in some instances keep her walking the floor night and day.
rubric_passages.Generalities: When one has been overdosed, as is done in a too prolonged proving, or by the crude drug, or when the prover has been markedly sensitive, we get an alternation of states, the internal alternating with the external. After the eruption is out the internal manifestations are not present, as is seen in the rheumatic state, the cough and the bowel symptoms.
rubric_passages.Head: Troublesome eczema of the scalp in infants, either purely vesicular or intermingled more or less with pustules. The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch.
rubric_passages.Rectum: pressure over the abdomen or pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool, and a feeling, with the expulsion of the stool, as if the rectum would protrude.
rubric_passages.Rectum, pain: pressure over the abdomen or pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool, and a feeling, with the expulsion of the stool, as if the rectum would protrude. Clinically it has been described as if the pain followed the intestines all the way down to the anus.
rubric_passages.Respiration: Attacks of violent coughing, with dyspnoea and choking, worse at night and worse on lying down, compelling him to sit up, to be bolstered up in bed, or to sit up in a reclining chair.
rubric_passages.Respiration, difficult: It has an asthmatic cough, coming on in the middle of the night, often arousing the patient from a sound sleep. Attacks of violent coughing, with dyspnoea and choking, worse at night and worse on lying down, compelling him to sit up, to be bolstered up in bed, or to sit up in a reclining chair.
rubric_passages.Skin: Croton oil, when applied to the skin, produces both vesicles and pustules upon an inflamed base, and the part becomes very red and sore. The inflammation often increases until it resembles erysipelas, but more commonly the eruption produced resembles a vesicular eczema.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Excessive nausea with vanishing of sight, vertigo, worse after drinking, with frequent discharges of yellowish-green water from the bowels; excessive nausea, much water in the mouth. So we note the excessive nausea and not so great vomiting.
rubric_passages.Abdomen, pain: With this the abdomen is very sensitive, and is greatly distended; there is much gurgling in the bowel. Another peculiar thing commonly attending Croton tig. diarrheas is that pressure over the abdomen or pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool.
rubric_passages.Clinical: If the diarrhoea becomes chronic the external eruption will disappear, and if the diarrhea improves the external eruption gets worse. It seems necessary in such a constitution to have a vent. The mucous membrane is but the internal skin, and the integument of the body the external skin, and this remedy especially manifests itself upon one or the other of these, the mucous membrane or the integument.
rubric_passages.Abdomen: With this the abdomen is very sensitive, and is greatly distended; there is much gurgling in the bowel, and when the physician puts his hand upon it the patient will say he feels the gurgling, as if he were full of water.
rubric_passages.Chest, pain: It feels to her as if a string were attached behind the nipple pulling backward, a sharp, drawing, stinging pain that will in some instances keep her walking the floor night and day.
rubric_passages.Face: The eruptions are often about the eyes, on the temples, over the face and on top of the head.
rubric_passages.Stool: one gush of yellow, watery or pappy stool; soft, thin faeces, coming, out with one gush, so marked is this that it is not an uncommon thing for a rural patient to describe it as "like that of a goose." It all gushes out in one squirt.
rubric_passages.Genitalia male: "Frequent, corrosive itching on glans and scrotum." "Vesicular eruption on scrotum and penis." It is a remedy for vesicular and pustular eruptions upon the genital organs.
rubric_passages.Stomach, vomiting: in Croton tig., the stools are copious and while there is nausea the vomiting is seldom and scanty.
rubric_passages.Head, eruption: Troublesome eczema of the scalp in infants, either purely vesicular or intermingled more or less with pustules. The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch. After desquamation has pretty nearly finished, a new crop of pustules and vesicles comes out, and while one place is clearing off another is vesicular.
rubric_passages.Head, eruption, crusts, scabs: The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch. After desquamation has pretty nearly finished, a new crop of pustules and vesicles comes out.
rubric_passages.Rectum, diarrhea: It is suitable in both acute and chronic diarrhoea. It is suitable in cholera infantum. The marked feature is the extreme, suddenness with which the stool is ejected.
rubric_passages.Skin, eruptions: the eruption produced resembles a vesicular eczema. This eruption will come on for a few days and will then desiccate, and in a few days longer it will desquamate.
rubric_passages.Stool, copious: in Croton tig., the stools are copious and while there is nausea the vomiting is seldom and scanty.
rubric_passages.Stool, thin, liquid: this violent gush of thin, yellow faeces or yellow water is a striking feature of the remedy.
rubric_passages.Head, eruption, crusts: The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch.
rubric_passages.Stool, thin: soft, thin faeces, coming, out with one gush
rubric_passages.Abdomen, gurgling: there is much gurgling in the bowel, and when the physician puts his hand upon it the patient will say he feels the gurgling, as if he were full of water, and it probably is so, for the expulsion of the stool would not occur in one strong gush were it not for the fact that the colon and rectum were full of fluid.
rubric_passages.Abdomen, pain, extending: Clinically it has been described as if the pain followed the intestines all the way down to the anus.

profile.personality: An intensely volatile, impulsive personality driven by an urgent need for action and control. These individuals are willful, accustomed to having their own way, and react with explosive violence—physical or emotional—when crossed or constrained. There is a fundamental tension between powerful inner forces and the attempt to contain them, manifesting as restlessness, nervous trembling, and a constant sense of being wound up.
profile.mental_state: The mind operates under extreme nervous tension with a tendency toward sudden, violent shifts—from coherent thought to wild delirium, from calm to maniacal fury, with startling abruptness. There is incoherent speech, loss of memory, and a characteristic pattern of mental suppression where blocking one outlet (a discharge, an emotion, a natural function) drives pathology violently to the brain. Impulsiveness dominates, with a compulsive feeling of needing to act, to fly, to do something dreadful.
profile.emotional_pattern: Emotions are spasmodic and convulsive rather than steady—alternating between spiteful violence, shrieking, weeping, and states of apparent emotional death or ecstasy. There is deep fear and anguish, particularly a terror of suffocation and imminent death, combined with hysterical reactivity to any form of suppression or constraint. The core pattern is one of emotions that cannot be smoothly expressed but instead build to explosive, uncontrollable release.

rubric_passages.Back: In the lower part of the chest, in the region of the xiphoid appendix, there is a spasmodic condition that is very troublesome. It seems to be at times a constriction so severe that he thinks he will die, and at others a feeling as if he were transfixed with a knife from the xiphoid appendix to the back.
rubric_passages.Back, pain: a feeling as if he were transfixed with a knife from the xiphoid appendix to the back.
rubric_passages.Chest: Cramping of the muscles of the chest; cramping of the calves; cramping all over. Spasms of the chest; spasms of the larynx; spasms of the whole respiratory system of such a character that the child seems to be choking to death. Constriction across the chest, suffocation, cramps of the legs.
rubric_passages.Chill: the extremities are cold, there is jerking of the muscles, cramping of the extremities and of the fingers and toes, spasms of the chest; he is cold, mottled, blue in blotches, going into collapse; the finger nails and toe nails and the hands and feet are blue.
rubric_passages.Extremities: In the fingers and toes and in the extremities the spasmodic condition increases and extends until the limbs are in a state of great exhaustion. Tonic contractions, the limbs being drawn up with great violence and it seems as if the frame would be torn to pieces by the violent contractions of the muscles everywhere.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness: the extremities are cold, there is jerking of the muscles, cramping of the extremities and of the fingers and toes, spasms of the chest; he is cold, mottled, blue in blotches, going into collapse; the finger nails and toe nails and the hands and feet are blue.
rubric_passages.Generalities: Convulsive cramps all over the body with twitching, jerking, trembling and blueness of the skin. Everything he does, all his actions are spasmodic, are convulsive. All the sphincters are convulsive, All the activities are irregular, disorderly and convulsive when poisoned with copper.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: In spasmodic conditions that come on during menstruation Cuprum is also useful. Painful menstruation with spasms commencing in the fingers and extending over the body. Tonic contractions that look like hysterical manifestations. Violent dysmenorrhoea with delirium, turning up of the eyes, contortions of the face and epileptiform manifestations.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female, pain: Before or during menses, or after suppression, violent, unbearable cramps in the abdomen. Painful menstruation with spasms commencing in the fingers and extending over the body.
rubric_passages.Head: Violent congestion in the head, violent pains in the head. Tingling pain in the vertex, severe pain in the vertex, bruised pain. Crawling sensation in vertex, stitches in the temples. Congestion of the brain, Meningitis. Headache after epileptic attacks.
rubric_passages.Mind: Cuprum has many mental symptoms. It has a great variety in its delirium, incoherent prattling, talking of all sorts of subjects incoherently. It has produced a variety of mental symptoms; delirium, incoherency of speech, loss of memory.
rubric_passages.Mind, restlessness, nervousness: In the Cuprum patient the nerves are all the time wrought up to the highest tension; wants to fly, wants to do something dreadful. Impulsiveness. Compelled to do something; restless and tossing about a constant uneasiness; nervous trembling; always tired.
rubric_passages.Mouth: Inflammation of the tongue. Paralysis of the tongue, It is not an uncommon thing to find paralysis in Cuprum after convulsions, The violence of the convulsions seems to have brought about a reaction and paralytic weakness, a numbness and tingling, a loss of motility.
rubric_passages.Rectum: In cholera morbus with gushing, watery stools and copious vomiting, the stomach and bowels are emptied of their contents. The patient is fairly emptied out.
rubric_passages.Respiration: Whenever the respiratory organs are affected there is spasmodic breathing, dyspnoea. There is also rattling in the chest. The more dyspnoea there is the more likely his thumbs will be clenched and the fingers cramped.
rubric_passages.Respiration, difficult: Spasmodic respiration; great dyspnea, asthmatic breathing. Attacks of spasmodic asthma and most violent spasmodic coughs. He is not able to take a full breath.
rubric_passages.Sleep: Twitching and jerking and starting during sleep. Grinding of the teeth with brain affections.
rubric_passages.Sleep, restless: Twitching and jerking and starting during sleep.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Spasms of the stomach. In the stomach and bowels periodical cramps. Cramps coming periodically. It has pain in the stomach, and a pain under the xiphoid appendix that seems as if it would take his life.
rubric_passages.Stomach, eructations: Some say it feels as if a lump were in that region and others as if much wind were collected in the stomach.
rubric_passages.Stomach, pain: Pressure in the stomach. It has pain in the stomach, and a pain under the xiphoid appendix that seems as if it would take his life. If it is not removed he will certainly die in a little while.
rubric_passages.Abdomen, pain: In young girls beginning to menstruate, violent cramps in the limbs, cramps in the abdomen, diarrhoea, cramps in the uterus. Before or during menses, or after suppression, violent, unbearable cramps in the abdomen.
rubric_passages.Bladder, urination: Convulsions with suppressed or scanty urine. No urine in the bladder.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: He is full of cramps and is compelled to cry out with the pain from the contractions of the muscles.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness: Great weakness of the muscles, and relaxation of the body when the convulsions are not on.
rubric_passages.Head, congestion: Violent congestion in the head, violent pains in the head. Congestion of the brain, Meningitis. Instead of convulsions it may take the form of congestion of the brain with violent delirium.
rubric_passages.Mind, restlessness: Compelled to do something; restless and tossing about a constant uneasiness; nervous trembling; always tired.
rubric_passages.Appetite: Great thirst for cold drinks. Desire for warm food and drinks. Eats hastily. Indigestion from milk.

profile.personality: A solitary, introspective individual who seeks warmth and seclusion, preferring to remain in a warm room alone with their thoughts. They are obstinate and censorious, yet fundamentally sensitive and easily overwhelmed, with a marked dullness of mind that prevents sustained mental effort.
profile.mental_state: Characterized by striking alternations: great flow of ideas alternates with weak memory, and dullness of mind prevents mental labor. The person becomes absorbed in thought, answers incoherently, and experiences confusion of mind, often ruminating about the future while seeking solitude.
profile.emotional_pattern: Joyous feelings alternate suddenly with irritability, and serene humor shifts abruptly into seriousness or peevishness. Grief and fear maintain a continuous mental agitation; she feels sadness as if she had wronged somebody, believes herself alone in the world and persecuted by everyone, and meditates tearfully upon imaginary grievances.

rubric_passages.Chill: Chill not ameliorated by warm clothing. Chilliness during menses. Chill forenoon or evening. Chill predominates in the evening. Heat of face follows chill. Chill and heat alternate.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Tearing, drawing pains in the limbs. Hyperesthesia of the skin. Flabby muscles. Tearing, drawing in the upper limbs. Sensation of weakness of the hands, as if she must let fall what she holds in her hands. Writer's cramp. Drawing pains in flexors of the leg. Burning sore pain in heels. Toes feel dead after walking. Weakness in the limbs.
rubric_passages.Generalities: Aversion to motion, yet motion ameliorates her pain and uneasiness. Weakness and aggravation from exertion. Flabby muscles. Most symptoms ameliorated by walking. Very restless at night. Great lassitude. Weakness in the evening, ameliorated by moving about. Sensitive to cold, and cold air. Complaints from being overheated.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: Menses too soon, or too late; irregular, or suppressed; copious, prolonged, or scanty. When profuse, the mental symptoms are better. Flow black and clotted. Labor-like pains at the menstrual period, commencing in small of back and running down each side of the pubes. Uterine haemorrhage. Suppressed menses from over-exertion, or being overheated. Fainting at the menstrual period. After menses, milk in mammae.

Pains worse lying on painful side or back. Pains one-sided. Pain morning and evening, ameliorated by vomiting; worse by motion and in open air.
rubric_passages.Mind: Alternations of moods is a striking feature of the mental state. Great flow of ideas alternates with weak memory. Joyous feeling alternating with irritability. Serene humor changes suddenly into seriousness or peevishness. Grief and fear keep her in continuous mental agitation. Dullness of mind prevents mental labor. Absorbed in thought seeks solitude; thinks about the future.
rubric_passages.Mouth: Taste is lost, or perverted; flat; bad; putrid; rancid; all food tastes too salty. Tongue white, or yellowish. Burning blisters on tongue. Saliva increased. Burning, tip of tongue. Saliva tastes salty. Viscid mucus in the mouth.

rubric_passages.Vertigo: Vertigo when walking in the open air; objects turn in a circle; ameliorated in a room, and when sitting. Everything turns dark before the eyes, and she falls as if fainting.

rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: Palpitation and anaemic murmurs. Tumultuous action of the heart; great lassitude. Weak pulse. Stitching in the chest and heart. Tearing, stitching, and shortness of breath during motion and rest.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female, menses, suppressed: Menses suppressed, palpitation of the heart, weeping, aversion to company and dread of open air. Suppressed menses from over-exertion, or being overheated.
rubric_passages.Head, pain: The pain in the head is stunning, and makes her fear she will lose her senses. Boring, darting, pressing in forehead and temples. Violent pains in the forehead. profile.personality: The Drosera constitution is a restless, anxious, and suspicious individual who dreads being alone yet distrusts even close friends. They are prone to feelings of persecution and haunted by nocturnal fears, particularly of ghosts, reflecting a deeply insecure and vigilant temperament.
profile.mental_state: The mind is marked by confusion, persistent suspicion, and a fixed sense of being persecuted. Sleeplessness and nocturnal anxiety dominate, with dread intensifying after midnight, producing a state of exhausted yet agitated wakefulness.
profile.emotional_pattern: Fear is the central emotional theme—fear of the dark, of solitude, and of betrayal by trusted companions. There is a deep-seated vulnerability expressed through clinging dependence combined with paranoid withdrawal, creating an isolating cycle of needing others yet being unable to trust them.
