profile.personality: The Dulcamara type is highly sensitive to environmental changes, particularly shifts from warm to cold and dry to damp conditions. They are restless and physically reactive individuals who thrive in stable, warm climates but become chronically ill when exposed to fluctuating temperatures. They tend toward a sickly, pallid, sallow constitution that deteriorates progressively with each exposure to adverse weather.
profile.mental_state: The key mental characteristic is a sluggish, dazed confusion that arises when chilled — the patient cannot remember, forgets what they were about to say, and loses the word that would naturally express their idea. There is a marked cognitive fog with suppressed perspiration, producing a state of mental torpor and impaired concentration. The mind becomes as congested and blocked as the catarrhal discharges that characterize the physical state.
profile.emotional_pattern: The Dulcamara patient carries an apprehensive vigilance about their own vulnerability, anticipating illness with each weather change — as Kent captures in the patient's words: 'I know I am fixed; I am now chilled.' There is a resigned weariness from chronic relapsing illness, a patient who has learned through repeated experience that every cold spell will rouse their symptoms anew. They are not dramatically emotional but rather stoically long-suffering, their distress expressed more through physical reactivity than overt emotional display.

rubric_passages.Back: Rheumatic lameness and stiffness in the back from taking cold better by motion. Drawing pain in the lumber region extending to the lower limbs during rest. Stiff neck from every exposure to cold.
rubric_passages.Back, pain: Rheumatic lameness and stiffness in the back from taking cold better by motion. Drawing pain in the lumber region extending to the lower limbs during rest.
rubric_passages.Bladder: In bladder catarrh, where there is a copious discharge of mucus, or muco-pus in the urine; when the urine stands, a thick, purulent sediment, yellowish-white, and a constant urging to urinate; every time he takes a little cold, the urine becomes bloody, the frequency of urination is increased, the urine becomes irritating, the catarrh of the bladder rouses up like a flame, all the symptoms are worse in cold, damp weather, and from getting chilled; better from becoming warm.
rubric_passages.Chest: The Dulcamara patient often becomes a sickly patient, with threatening of the catarrhal discharges to centre in the bronchial tubes, i.e., in the mucous membrane of the breathing apparatus. Many adults die of acute phthisis that might have been cured by Dulcamara.
rubric_passages.Chill: fevers may come on, aching in the bones, trembling with the aching, trembling in the muscles, and as the fever goes on, he is in a distressed state... It suits these colds that have this sluggish circulation of the brain, with trembling and chilliness, coldness as if in the bones.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Stitching, tearing, rheumatic pains in limbs after exposure to cold, better by motion, worse at night or in the evening, with some fever. Sore, bruised feeling all over the body. Warts on hands, fingers and face.
rubric_passages.Generalities: The Dulcamara patient is disturbed by every change in the weather, from warm to cold, from dry to moist, and from suddenly cooling the body while perspiring. He is ameliorated in dry, even weather; cold and damp aggravate all the conditions. He is worse evening and night and during rest.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: Rash comes out upon the face before the menses. As a forerunner of catamenia, with extraordinary sexual excitement, herpetic eruptions.
rubric_passages.Head: Catarrhal and rheumatic headaches in cold, damp weather. One who is subject to Dulcamara headaches, has the dry catarrh; whenever he takes cold instead of the usual catarrhal flow with it, he at first sneezes and then feels a dryness in the air passages, a slacking up of the usual discharge, which would give him relief, and then he knows that he must look out, for along will come the neuralgic pains, pains in the occiput, and finally over the whole head. Congestive headaches, with neuralgic pains and dry nose.
rubric_passages.Mind: cannot remember, forgets what he was about to speak of, forgets the word that would naturally express his idea, and he enters into a dazed state, a state of confusion.
rubric_passages.Mouth: Its cold sores are very troublesome. The patients are subject to these cold sores upon the lips and upon the genitals. Every time he takes cold, herpes labialis, herpes preputialis.
rubric_passages.Rectum: It is a medicine wonderfully useful in diarrhoea, at the close of the summer, hot days and cold nights, with changeable stool; diarrhoea of infants. There seems to be no digestion; yellow, slimy stool; yellow-green stool, with undigested stool; frequent stool, blood in the stool and quite a mass of slime, showing a marked catarrhal state.
rubric_passages.Respiration: Cough, from damp, cold, atmosphere, or from getting wet. Cough, dry, hoarse and rough, or loose, with copious expectoration of mucus and dull hearing; catarrhal fever. The cough is worse lying and in a warm room and better in the open air.
rubric_passages.Skin: Another feature of this medicine is its tendency to throw out eruptions over the body. It is a wonderfully eruptive medicine, producing vesicles, crusts, dry, brown crusts, humid crusts, herpes. Dulcamara produces eruptions so nearly like impetigo that it has been found a useful remedy in that condition. i.e., multiple little boil-like eruptions; it produces little boils, and the boils spread.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Dulcamara produces catarrh of the stomach, intestines, nose, eyes, ears, and inflammatory conditions of the skin with eruptions.
rubric_passages.Bladder, urination: Doctor, if I get chilled, I must hurry to urinate; if I get into a cold place, I have to go to stool, or to urinate. So we see that the symptoms come on when the patient is cold, and are better when he is warm. Any catarrhal trouble of the bladder that is better in the summer and worse in the winter.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: Dulcamara is full of rheumatism, full of rheumatic pains and aches, sore and bruised all over, the joints are inflamed, become red, sensitive to touch and are swollen. Sore, bruised feeling all over the body.
rubric_passages.Clinical: There is a form of acute Bright's disease that Dulcamara cures. In cases of Bright's disease following scarlet fever, or from malaria, or in any acute disease that has ended badly, i.e., the patient has been exposed to the cold too soon, and has taken cold, or from sudden change of weather, damp and cold, the feet commence to swell, there is albumin in the urine, the limbs are waxy, the face becomes waxy and sallow, and there is constant urging to urinate.
rubric_passages.Abdomen: Chronic recurrent dysentery from cold. If they have a dose of Dulcamara it fortifies them against the continual taking of cold.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: It suits these colds that have this sluggish circulation of the brain, with trembling and chilliness, coldness as if in the bones.
rubric_passages.Chest, pain: Mammae engorged, hard, sore and painful. Mammary glands swollen, inactive, painless, itching, in consequence of a cold which seems to have settled in them.

profile.personality: Euphrasia presents a chilly, sensitive constitution prone to acute catarrhal states, particularly affecting the eyes and upper respiratory tract. The patient is reactive to environmental stimuli — wind, cold air, and open air provoke symptoms — suggesting a delicate, easily overwhelmed temperament that seeks shelter and warmth.
profile.mental_state: The mental picture is not strongly developed in Kent's lecture, indicating Euphrasia acts primarily on the physical plane of acute illness rather than deep constitutional mental states. The head symptoms suggest a mind clouded and burdened by catarrhal congestion, with a bruised, bursting quality to the headache and dazzling sensitivity to light that impairs clear thinking.
profile.emotional_pattern: The emotional pattern reflects irritability and restlessness driven by intense physical discomfort — constant burning, itching, and smarting in the eyes compel rubbing and winking, suggesting a state of agitated distress. There is an overall sensitivity and vulnerability characteristic of acute suffering, with the patient unable to find comfort in the open air and struggling to get warm, pointing to a withdrawn, self-protective emotional posture during illness.

rubric_passages.Chest: Abundant secretion in the larynx causing loose cough with rattling in the chest.
rubric_passages.Chill: There is chill, fever, and sweat in this remedy. This chill predominates.
rubric_passages.Generalities: Euphrasia is a short acting remedy of great usefulness in acute catarrhal affections with or without fever.
rubric_passages.Head: Headaches that occur with coryza and eye symptoms, head aches in the evening as if bruised. Stitching pain in the head.
rubric_passages.Skin: The remedy has a rash like measles and it has febrile symptoms.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: He is a chilly patient and cannot get warm in bed.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain, burning: Sensation of dryness, burning, biting in the eyes.
rubric_passages.Clinical: It is especially suitable in catarrhal fever, influenza and measles.
rubric_passages.Face: The fever occurs mostly during the day with red face and cold hands.
rubric_passages.Head, pain: Headaches; as if the head would burst with dazzling of the eyes from sunlight. These are catarrhal headaches with profuse watery discharge from the eyes and nose.
rubric_passages.Eye: Catarrh condition of the eyes with copious, acrid, watery discharge with or without coryza. Cutting pain in the eyes extending into the head, pressure in the eyes as if caused by sand. Sensation of dryness, burning, biting in the eyes.
rubric_passages.Eye, lachrymation: Violent itching of the eyes obliging rubbing and winking, with copious lachrymation. Copious, acrid lachrymation with fluent discharge from the nose during coryza.
rubric_passages.Eye, pain: Cutting pain in the eyes extending into the head, pressure in the eyes as if caused by sand. Sensation of dryness, burning, biting in the eyes.
rubric_passages.Eye, redness: Pupils much contracted and much tumefaction of the mucous membrane with redness and enlarged blood vessels and smarting.
rubric_passages.Eye, swollen: Much swelling of the lids with inflammation. Fine rash about the eyes with puffiness of the lids.
rubric_passages.Eye, swollen, lids: The lids are very sensitive and swollen. Dryness of the lids and the margins of the lids red swollen, and burning.
rubric_passages.Larynx and trachea: Hoarseness in the morning. Irritation in the larynx compelling him to cough, followed by pressure beneath the sternum. Abundant secretion in the larynx causing loose cough with rattling in the chest.
rubric_passages.Vision: Blurred vision. Paralysis of the third nerve.
rubric_passages.Vision, dim: Blurred vision. Amblyopia with inflammation of conjunctiva and lids.
rubric_passages.Eye, lid: agglutination of the lids in the morning. Dryness of the lids and the margins of the lids red swollen, and burning. The margins of the lids itch and burn. Suppuration of the margins of the lids.
rubric_passages.Cough: Violent cough from tickling in the larynx. No cough at night causes the remedy to resemble Bry. and Mang. The dyspnoea and cough are ameliorated by lying down.
rubric_passages.Expectoration: Cough with copious expectoration along with or following coryza. The expectoration is easy and almost without cough. It comes up without much effort.
rubric_passages.Eye, inflammation: General inflammation of all the tissues of the eyes. Ulceration of the cornea. It has cured Pannus. Pustular inflammation.
rubric_passages.Eye, inflammation, conjunctiva: It is suitable in the most violent acute conjunctivitis. Amblyopia with inflammation of conjunctiva and lids.
rubric_passages.Fever: The fever occurs mostly during the day with red face and cold hands. The heat descends the body.
rubric_passages.Nose: Sneezing and fluent coryza. The discharge is bland and this occurs with acrid lachrymation. The nasal mucous membrane is swollen. Profuse, bland, fluent, coryza.

profile.personality: A weak, anemic individual who appears deceptively healthy due to facial flushing, yet is fundamentally flabby, relaxed, and incapable of endurance. They are oversensitive and excitable, craving gentle movement but collapsing under any real exertion or sudden effort.
profile.mental_state: The mind is confused, weary, and depressed, with the highest degree of despondency and difficulty concentrating. Mental effort such as writing brings on congestive headaches, and any hurry or sudden stimulus causes blackness before the eyes and dizziness.
profile.emotional_pattern: Tearful, irritable, and anxious from the slightest cause, with extreme nervous sensitivity where even minor noises like crackling paper provoke agitation. They suffer deeply from lack of sympathy, as their flushed appearance belies their genuine inner weakness and distress.

rubric_passages.Bladder: The bladder is also relaxed. Its sphincter is weak, and there is no regularity of its muscular action. Hence, we have involuntary urination from sudden motion, from walking, or from coughing.
rubric_passages.Chest: Difficult respiration; pains and disturbances in the chest. Difficult breathing, with a sense of a great load on the chest. Suffocating fits at night; catarrhal conditions of the respiratory tract; congestion of the chest; dyspnoea.
rubric_passages.Chill: Evening chill or chilliness with fever, cold hands and feet and red face. Icy cold feet with the chill. Chill ameliorated after eating. Thirst with the chill.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Rheumatic pains: in the extremities, ameliorated by heat and by gentle motion; aggravated by cold, by exertion, or by rapid motion. Tearing pains through the limbs. Inability to raise the arm; paralytic pains, - that is, pains that are benumbing.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness: Coldness of the limbs; and again, heat of the soles and palms, they change about.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness, hands: Evening chill or chilliness with fever, cold hands and feet and red face.
rubric_passages.Generalities: Any rapid exertion or motion brings on weakness, dyspnea, sinking and fainting. A strange thing running through all the constitutional conditions of Ferrum is that the pains and sufferings come on during rest. The patient is ameliorated by moving gently about, but any exertion tires and causes faintness.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness, enervation: Weakness and prostration; weakness even from talking. Prostration with irregular pulse and rapid pulse, or with too slow pulse; palpitation. And then comes paralytic weakness; the limbs give out.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: Weakness and relaxation of the genital organs is common to Ferrum. The menstrual flow comes in for its share. Copious, watery flow; haemorrhage or suppression - amenorrhoea - no flow at all, only a leucorrhoea. Suppression of the menses with great nervous excitement; with flushed face; with weakness and palpitation.
rubric_passages.Head: The headaches are congestive in character, with mounting of blood upwards. There is a sense of fullness and distension, with red face. Throbbing like hammers in the head. Every quick motion aggravates the headache.
rubric_passages.Mind: The mind is confused and the patient tearful. Depression of spirits; mental weariness and depression. The highest degree of depression and despondency. Anxiety from the slightest cause; irritability.
rubric_passages.Mind, irritability: The least noise, like the crackling of paper, sets the patient wild. It brings on nervous excitement and restlessness; she must get up and move. Excitement from the slightest opposition.
rubric_passages.Mind, restlessness, nervousness: Restless when keeping still; must keep the limbs moving. The least noise, like the crackling of paper, sets the patient wild. It brings on nervous excitement and restlessness; she must get up and move.
rubric_passages.Mouth: The tongue feels as if burnt.
rubric_passages.Rectum: Relaxation runs through the remedy. From this relaxation there is prolapsus of the rectum, vagina and uterus. Dragging down in the lower part of the body, as if the organs would come out-and sometimes they do come out.
rubric_passages.Respiration: Difficult respiration; pains and disturbances in the chest. Difficult breathing, with a sense of a great load on the chest. Suffocating fits at night; catarrhal conditions of the respiratory tract; congestion of the chest; dyspnoea.
rubric_passages.Respiration, difficult: Difficult breathing, with a sense of a great load on the chest. Suffocating fits at night.
rubric_passages.Skin: The skin pits upon pressure and is pale, yet the face shows an appearance of plethora. The patient becomes greenish, waxy, yellow and pallid, with a sickly and anemic countenance.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Nothing taken into the stomach digests, and yet there is no special nausea. Food goes into the stomach and is vomited without nausea-simply emptied out. It is like a leather bag; it will not digest anything. Fill it up and it empties itself just as easily as it was filled.
rubric_passages.Appetite, increased (hunger in general): Canine hunger. Double the amount of an ordinary meal in the evening was hardly sufficient.
rubric_passages.Appetite, ravenous, canine, excessive: Canine hunger. Double the amount of an ordinary meal in the evening was hardly sufficient.
rubric_passages.Appetite, aversion, acids, to: Sweet wines agree, but sour wines and all sour things disagree.
rubric_passages.Stomach, eructations: After eating there are eructations. Heat in the stomach; regurgitation of food. Sometimes there are eructations of food by the mouthful, like Phosphorus.
rubric_passages.Vertigo: Any sudden or rapid motion, or the least hurry, causes blackness before the eyes; dizziness; things turn in a circle; she must sit down.
rubric_passages.Bladder, urination: Hence, we have involuntary urination from sudden motion, from walking, or from coughing. In little children the urine dribbles all day just as long as the child plays the urine dribbles and keeps the clothing wet, but this better while keeping perfectly quiet.
rubric_passages.Generalities, pain: A strange thing running through all the constitutional conditions of Ferrum is that the pains and sufferings come on during rest. The patient is ameliorated by moving gently about, but any exertion tires and causes faintness. Any rapid motion aggravates the complaints.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness: All the muscular fibers become tired from any exertion. Rapid exercise, or, any unusual exertion is impossible. Any rapid exertion or motion brings on weakness, dyspnea, sinking and fainting. Weakness and prostration; weakness even from talking.

profile.personality: A restless yet exhausted individual who craves solitude and is averse to company, work, and mental effort. They alternate between unusual mental clarity with abundant ideas and complete indifference to pleasure and exciting events. Despite great lassitude compelling them to lie down, they are ameliorated by gentle, slow motion.
profile.mental_state: Marked difficulty concentrating and reflecting on ordinary questions, with confusion of mind especially in the morning and evening, ameliorated by washing the face in cold water. The mind alternates between states of unusual clearness and abundant ideas, and periods of stupefaction, forgetfulness, and complete aversion to thinking or mental work. There is a strong tendency toward dissatisfaction with possessions and surroundings, with obstinacy and moroseness.
profile.emotional_pattern: Prone to intense anger even to the point of violence, producing weakness, headache, trembling, and sweat. Deep anxiety pervades, especially at night, as if having done a great wrong to someone, with fears of death, crowds, misfortune, and apoplexy. Mood alternates unpredictably between cheerful, talkative hilarity and profound evening sadness, with underlying hypochondriacal worry and hysterical sensitivity to noise.

rubric_passages.Chest: Anxiety in chest and region of the heart. Catarrh of the chest. Congestion of the chest. Constriction of chest and heart. Sensation of fullness. Haemorrhage of lungs. Heat.
rubric_passages.Extremities: Cold extremities. Cold hands and feet. Cold feet evening in bed. Cold feet during headache. Contraction of fingers, result of rheumatism. Cramps in thighs, legs, calve, feet.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness: Cold extremities. Cold hands and feet. Cold feet evening in bed. Cold feet during headache.
rubric_passages.Generalities: Great weakness, and desire to lie down. Nervous at night. Rheumatic conditions.
rubric_passages.Stomach: Appetite diminished. Ravenous appetite without relish of food. Appetite entirely gone. Aversion to food, meat, milk. Desires sour things. Distension of stomach after eating.
rubric_passages.Stomach, eructations: Eructations, after eating, better, empty, of food, foul, sour. Water-brash.
rubric_passages.Clinical: While it has been used by Schuessler's followers for the first stage of inflammatory fevers, it is useful in the higher potencies in chronic diseases, and is a deep acting anti-psoric.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: Palpitation at night with anxiety, on exertion and motion, while sitting, walking rapidly.
rubric_passages.Ear: Purulent discharge from the ear. Itching in ear. Noises in the ear; roaring, buzzing, humming, ringing and singing. Catarrh of Eustachian tubes. Inflammatory pains in the ear. Otitis media. Pain deep in the ear. Drawing. Stitching. Pain and swelling of the parotid glands. Sensitive to noise. Impaired hearing.
rubric_passages.Face: Chlorotic face. Dark circles under the eyes. Earthy, pale, sallow face. Pale lips. Red face alternating with paleness. Circumscribed redness of cheeks. Red during fever; during headache. Yellow. Liver spots.
rubric_passages.Throat: Constriction of throat. Redness in throat and tonsils. Swollen tonsils. Heat in throat. Inflammation of throat and tonsils. Lumps in throat. Pain on swallowing. Burning. Soreness.
rubric_passages.Chest, lungs: Inflammation of bronchial tubes, lungs and pleura; oppression of chest.
rubric_passages.Extremities, pain: Rheumatic pain in right shoulder and upper arm, of a drawing, tearing character, aggravated by violent motion of arm, ameliorated by gentle motion (Ferr.), part sensitive to touch.
rubric_passages.Face, discoloration, pale: Earthy, pale, sallow face. Pale lips.
rubric_passages.Face, discoloration: Chlorotic face. Dark circles under the eyes. Earthy, pale, sallow face. Pale lips. Red face alternating with paleness. Circumscribed redness of cheeks. Red during fever; during headache. Yellow. Liver spots.
rubric_passages.Expectoration: Expectoration in daytime, morning, bloody, bright red, dark, copious, difficult, frothy, greenish, mucus, offensive, purulent, scanty, putrid, thick, viscid, whitish, yellow.
rubric_passages.Expectoration, bloody, spitting of blood: Expectoration in daytime, morning, bloody, bright red, dark, copious.
rubric_passages.Expectoration, bloody: Expectoration in daytime, morning, bloody, bright red, dark, copious.
rubric_passages.Chest, inflammation, bronchial tubes (br: Inflammation of bronchial tubes, lungs and pleura.
rubric_passages.Chest, phthisis pulmonalis: This is a valuable temporary remedy in the acute colds during the course of phthisis. In acute phthisis.
rubric_passages.Fever: The fever predominates. Fever at any time with inflammation of organs, joints, or mucus membranes. Fever without chill. Dry heat with thirst. Flushes of heat. Hectic fever and night sweats. Internal heat. Remittent fever. Heat after sleep.
rubric_passages.Nose: Catarrh of nose. Coryza; discharge bloody. Crusts form in the nose. Discharge excoriating, purulent.
rubric_passages.Perspiration: Perspiration in daytime, morning; clammy; with great weakness, on slight exertion, following the fever; copious; during sleep.
rubric_passages.Perspiration, clammy: Perspiration in daytime, morning; clammy; with great weakness, on slight exertion, following the fever; copious; during sleep.
rubric_passages.Chest, inflammation: Inflammation of bronchial tubes, lungs and pleura; oppression of chest.
rubric_passages.Clinical, inflammation: Fever at any time with inflammation of organs, joints, or mucus membranes.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, pulse: Strong, full, frequent pulse. Strong pulsation over body, and in head.

profile.personality: A deeply debilitated, restless individual who craves constant stimulation and novelty, moving from one indulgence to the next. Hot-blooded and aggravated by warmth, they are driven by insatiable appetites—for food, for sensation, for change—yet find only fleeting satisfaction. They tend toward overwork or excess until mental and physical exhaustion sets in.
profile.mental_state: Mental exhaustion and depression from prolonged overwork or dissipation, with a brain fatigued to the point of dullness and silence. May fall into profound reticence, sitting quietly and answering nothing, or into a state of low-mindedness where higher faculties of judgment and self-control have deteriorated. The mind is slow, insidious in its decline, mirroring the remedy's deep and gradual action.
profile.emotional_pattern: Marked indifference toward loved ones—an alienation from spouse, children, and closest friends, with aversion to home life and domestic responsibilities. Restless dissatisfaction drives them away from what should bring comfort, while overwhelming sexual erethism and uncontrollable desire may dominate the emotional landscape. There is a deep melancholy and sadness underlying the apparent indifference, reflecting a fundamental loss of orderly affection and human connection.

rubric_passages.Back: It is suitable after Sil. in the spinal affections that are attended with paralysis, trembling and numbness in the soles of the feet. It will often stop the progress of structural nervous diseases and prevent the, case from getting worse.
rubric_passages.Bladder: suffers if the call to urinate cannot be immediately attended to, hence, as in the text, "headaches > by micturition."
rubric_passages.Extremities: The feet burn and are put out of bed in the night; he hunts around in bed for a cool place for the feet and bands. The soles perspire, and the palms perspire, and the sweat is acrid, making the parts sore; excoriation from the sweat between the toes.
rubric_passages.Generalities: It takes a long time for this remedy, in the proving, to develop its symptoms. It is a very deep-acting medicine, and an antipsoric, antisyphilitic and anti-sycotic. It is insidious in its action and its symptoms are slow in approach; it is like the deepest and slowest and most tedious diseases, the miasms, and hence it is suitable in the very slowest and lowest forms of disease.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: The vulva is eaten off and the tonsils become honeycombed by syphilitic ulcers.
rubric_passages.Head: Violent congestive headache with beat and fullness. Violent occipital headaches, worse from motion.
rubric_passages.Mouth: The teeth decay or break off or ulcerate at the roots; fistulous openings from the root of the tooth, continuing to discharge. Many a time has this remedy taken away that ulcer of the root, closed up that fistulous opening, cured the pain, saved the tooth.
rubric_passages.Rectum: Hemorrhoids protrude after a stool; the anus and rectum protrude, and there is some bleeding, because of the hemorrhoidal condition.
rubric_passages.Skin: Whenever such is the case, we know that a remedy has great depth of action and that it is very long acting. It forms like incrustations here and there upon the skin that seem to have no tendency to heal. A crust forms, but there seems to be no healing beneath the crust.
rubric_passages.Bladder, urination: If the call to urinate be not attended to the headache will continue to grow < until the urine is avoided. That is a peculiar symptom, and it sometimes leads to the study of Fluor, ac.
rubric_passages.Clinical: Fluoric acid will prevent the manifestation of disease in sycotic subjects, will prevent formation of fig warts. It cures fig warts. It produces hardened, dry warts, and dry crusts upon the skin, and crusts not unlike rupia. It is useful in syphilitic rupia.
rubric_passages.Abdomen: Warm, drinks bring on a diarrhea, or flatulence, or disturbance in the stomach, and cause indigestion to manifest itself in various way
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: From the feebleness of the circulation one might suppose that numbness would naturally be present, and it is true.
rubric_passages.Ear: Bone affections stand out prominently. Necrosis, especially of the long bones, but also of the bones of the ear. It creates an offensive acrid discharge from the ear.
rubric_passages.Ear, pain: Caries of the temporal bone; discharges offensive smelling pus periodically.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat, sensation of: Sensation as if a burning vapor were emitted from the pores of the body.
rubric_passages.Head, pain: headaches > by micturition. That symptom is all that is given in the text; but remember something that is analogous to it, viz.; If the call to urinate be not attended to the headache will continue to grow < until the urine is avoided.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat: In the evening and night great heat seems to evolve from the body without increase of temperature. The skin becomes very hot.
rubric_passages.Generalities, bones: Think of the remedy, then, in vicious bone diseases, in necrosis and caries, in fistulous openings, fistula leading to the teeth, fistula lachrymalis and fistula in ano; in calcareous degenerations; in deformity of the nails, hair and teeth; in affections of the thigh bones and leg bones, with chronic fistulous openings leading to bone discharging pus which excoriates the parts all around.
rubric_passages.Eye: whole left side of the head retarded in growth, left eye seems smaller.
rubric_passages.Eye, lachrymation: an acrid lachrymation or other discharge from the eye
rubric_passages.Genitalia male: He is kept awake nights by erections. This state of desire forces itself upon him, not only when he is with the opposite sex, but at all times. At times, in the beginning of a gonorrhea, this condition of priapism and intense uncontrollable sexual desire, with swelling of the foreskin is overcome by Fluoric acid.

profile.personality: A slow, heavy, sluggish constitution with profound muscular weakness and a paralytic quality pervading the entire system. The Gelsemium type is drowsy, dull, and desires to be left alone, lying still not from aversion to motion as in Bryonia, but from sheer inability to move due to overwhelming heaviness and fatigue.
profile.mental_state: The mind is dazed, stupid, and forgetful, with an inability to think in an orderly way; the patient talks incoherently as if delirious. There is a characteristic mental fog where one lies awake thinking yet thinks of nothing in particular, the mind refusing to function with any coordination. Confusion of vision and sensation accompany the mental dullness, with numbness and loss of awareness spreading through the body.
profile.emotional_pattern: Anticipatory anxiety and fright dominate the emotional picture, with physical symptoms triggered by fear, embarrassment, shock, and sudden surprises — producing involuntary discharges, palpitation, faintness, and exhaustion. There is a hysterical, nervous element with states of high excitement alternating with profound prostration and trembling. The patient becomes overwhelmed by upcoming events or social encounters, unable to resist opposing circumstances, collapsing into weakness.

rubric_passages.Back: There are also paralytic weakness of the extremities and of the hands. With paralytic states there is aching along the spine and in the muscles of the back; drawing, cramping in the muscles of the back and aching under the left shoulder blade.
rubric_passages.Back, coldness: Great coldness running up the back from the lower part of the spine to the back of the head. Shuddering, as if ice were rubbed up the back.
rubric_passages.Back, pain: With paralytic states there is aching along the spine and in the muscles of the back; drawing, cramping in the muscles of the back and aching under the left shoulder blade.
rubric_passages.Back, pain, dorsal region: With paralytic states there is aching along the spine and in the muscles of the back; drawing, cramping in the muscles of the back and aching under the left shoulder blade.
rubric_passages.Back, pain, dorsal region, scapulae: aching under the left shoulder blade.
rubric_passages.Bladder: there is involuntary loss of stool and urine.
rubric_passages.Bladder, urination, frequent: The headache is relieved by passing a copious quantity of urine; that is, the urine which has probably been scanty becomes free and then the headache subsides.
rubric_passages.Chest: There is a sense of weakness and goneness in the region of the heart, and this weakness and goneness often extend into the stomach, involving the whole lower part of the left side of the chest and across the stomach, creating a sensation of hunger, like Ignatia and Sepia.
rubric_passages.Chill: In congestive chill with high temperature occurring in the afternoon, the chill part of it subsiding and the fever becoming continued, Gels. is a useful remedy.
rubric_passages.Chill, trembling and shivering: Shuddering, as if ice were rubbed up the back.
rubric_passages.Extremities: there is one grand feature, viz., a feeling of great weight and tiredness in the entire body and limbs.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness: Coldness of the fingers and toes; sometimes the extremities are icy cold to the knees, while the head is hot and the face purple.
rubric_passages.Extremities, paralysis: There is also paralytic weakness of the extremities and of the hands.
rubric_passages.Extremities, paralysis, painless: The muscles are relaxed, they do not hold the lids up. The lids close when he is looking steadily; they simply fall down over the eyes.
rubric_passages.Generalities: running through the febrile complaints, in the spinal meningitis, in congestion of the brain, in intermittents or remittents that change to a continued fever, and even in a cold when the patient is sneezing and has hot face and red eyes, there is one grand feature, viz., a feeling of great weight and tiredness in the entire body and limbs.
rubric_passages.Generalities, weakness, enervation: It seems that he is unable to move, that he is too weak to move, and this runs through all complaints.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female: The symptoms of Gels. may be present in inflammation of any organ, uterus or ovaries, stomach, the lungs and of the rectum.
rubric_passages.Genitalia female, pain: The symptoms of Gels. may be present in inflammation of any organ, uterus or ovaries, stomach, the lungs and of the rectum.
rubric_passages.Head: The headaches are of the congestive type. The most violent pain is in the occiput, and it is felt sometimes as a hammering. Every pulsation is felt like the blow of a hammer in the base of the skull.
rubric_passages.Mind: The patient feels dazed and talks as if he were delirious; incoherent, stupid, forgetful.
rubric_passages.Mouth: The tongue also becomes paralyzed and does not perform its work in an orderly way.
rubric_passages.Mouth, dryness: a continued fever extends from one paroxysm into another, with a higher temperature in the afternoon. The chill practically subsides, leaving a state which has the appearance of typhoid, with dry tongue, not much thirst and marked head symptoms, dazed in mind.
rubric_passages.Rectum: A soldier going into battle has an involuntary stool; involuntary discharges from fright and surprises accompanying fright.
rubric_passages.Skin: In a most distressing and violent, rapidly spreading erysipelas that seems destined to cause death in a few days all the symptoms point to Gels., and though Gels. may not have produced erysipelas, it will stop the progress of the disease in a few hours and the patient will go on to a quick recovery.
rubric_passages.Sleep: The sleep is greatly disturbed. He cannot go to sleep; every excitement keeps him awake. During marked febrile conditions he has a profound sleep or coma.
rubric_passages.Stomach: There is a sense of weakness and goneness in the region of the heart, and this weakness and goneness often extend into the stomach, involving the whole lower part of the left side of the chest and across the stomach, creating a sensation of hunger, like Ignatia and Sepia.
rubric_passages.Stomach, thirst: The patient in general is thirstless, and it is the exception that there is much thirst.
rubric_passages.Vertigo: The patient feels dazed and talks as if he were delirious; incoherent, stupid, forgetful.
rubric_passages.Back, coldness, extending: Great coldness running up the back from the lower part of the spine to the back of the head.

profile.personality: The Glonoinum type is a plethoric individual who is highly sensitive to heat, light, and environmental stimulation. They are easily overwhelmed by exertion and tend toward a sedentary disposition, sitting perfectly still for long periods to avoid aggravating their condition.
profile.mental_state: Marked by confusion and disorientation, particularly during episodes of cerebral congestion — they may lose their way near home and fail to recognize familiar faces. Mental application is difficult or impossible during acute states, with trembling that prevents writing or fine work, progressing in chronic cases toward softening of the brain.
profile.emotional_pattern: The dominant emotional tone is one of frantic distress driven by intense physical suffering, sometimes violent enough to provoke desperate acts like attempting to jump from windows. Between episodes they are apprehensive and avoidant, organizing their life around preventing the next surge of congestion, carrying umbrellas and avoiding warmth, wine, and stimulants.

rubric_passages.Back: Some months after being violently jarred by being thrown from a carriage, a sensitiveness of the upper part of the back and neck came on.
rubric_passages.Chest: a feeling as if all the blood in the body must be rushing around the heart, with a sense of heat or a boiling sensation in the region of the heart, or in the left side of the chest.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, palpitation: With this congestion of the head there is commonly vomiting, palpitation of the heart, pain in the stomach, great difficulty in breathing and finally loss of consciousness.
rubric_passages.Extremities: The pulsations are tremendous and when they are greatest in the head they are felt also in the extremities. The fingers and toes pulsate, there is pulsation throughout the back, and it seems that the whole body throbs.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness: In the extremities we have great coldness. The extremities cold, pale and perspiring, the head hot and the face flushed and purple or bright red.
rubric_passages.Extremities, coldness, hands: while walking on the street he feels a surging to the brain like a flush of heat and a flush on the face, his hands tremble, and the hands and feet become cold, he breaks out in a sweat
rubric_passages.Generalities: There are some persons that are apparently plethoric; very much affected by the slightest exertion and who have pulsation all over; pulsation in a warm room.
rubric_passages.Generalities, faintness, fainting: any little exertion or excitement brings on the rush of blood to the heart and fainting spells; fainting, spells in women, who are not supposed to be subject to fainting.
rubric_passages.Head: The most common feature in this remedy is the surging of blood to the head and to the heart. A patient often describes the state as a feeling as if all the blood in the body must be rushing around the heart, with a sense of heat or a boiling sensation in the region of the heart, or in the left side of the chest.
rubric_passages.Mind: All degrees of confusion of mind, even loss of consciousness, will be present.
rubric_passages.Stomach: With this congestion of the head there is commonly vomiting, palpitation of the heart, pain in the stomach, great difficulty in breathing and finally loss of consciousness.
rubric_passages.Generalities, faintness: any little exertion or excitement brings on the rush of blood to the heart and fainting spells; fainting, spells in women, who are not supposed to be subject to fainting. Great weakness, palpitation, trembling of the limbs, shaking of one or both hands as with palsy.
rubric_passages.Head, congestion: Glonoine corresponds to congestive states in the head that come on suddenly, especially from heat, but also from gaslight, or from any bright light.
rubric_passages.Clinical: Children get sick in the night after sitting up at an open fire or falling asleep there.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, heart: Laborious action of the heart is a strong feature of the remedy; pulsation all over.
rubric_passages.Head, vertex: Vertex burning hot, likewise upper part of back.
rubric_passages.Back, dorsal region, back: The burning heat seems to appear at the top of the head and extend down between the shoulders; a sensation of heat, as from a band.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation: Fluttering in the region of the heart. Pulse quick, irregular, slow or quick and wiry.
rubric_passages.Back, dorsal region: Hot, especially in the back of the neck and between the shoulders.
rubric_passages.Face: Face bluish, with a heavy, stupid expression.
rubric_passages.Head, heat: The whole crown of the head feels as if it were covered by a hot iron, as if an oven were close by.
rubric_passages.Head, pain: every pulsation is painful, so that there are painful pulsations and sometimes painless pulsations.
rubric_passages.Generalities, heat: It is noticeable also that Glonoine symptoms are worse in the heat of summer and relieved in winter. The dull headaches and the continuous headaches are aggravated from warm weather and ameliorated from cold.
rubric_passages.Mind, dullness: he looks around him and does not know which way to go home, he does not know where his dwelling is. He looks in the faces of friends and they seem strange, he loses his way when be is near home. It is a confusion which soon passes away, and he feels better again.
rubric_passages.Heart & Circulation, palpitation, tumult: Persons who are subject to palpitation with dyspnea, upon any effort, he cannot go uphill, he cannot walk along the pavement without bringing on palpitation and dyspnea; any little exertion or excitement brings on the rush of blood to the heart and fainting spells
rubric_passages.Eye: Red eyes, with photophobia; optical illusions. Black specks before the eyes; blindness.
rubric_passages.Eye, injected: The pupils are dilated and the eyes red.
rubric_passages.Eye, redness: The pupils are dilated and the eyes red.
rubric_passages.Clinical, convulsions: Convulsions come on, convulsions throughout all the limbs, the neck and whole body drawn back, opisthotonos.
